# Cloudflare CDN & Caching Proxy Setup Guide

This guide provides step-by-step instructions to configure a **Cloudflare Worker** as a CDN proxy for your **Backblaze B2** bucket. This setup runs on Cloudflare's free tier, requires **no custom domain**, and incurs **zero egress fees** from Backblaze B2 (via the Bandwidth Alliance).

---

## 1. Local Backend Configuration
Ensure you configure your backend project's `.env` file with the CDN URL:
```env
# The URL generated by Step 2 below (e.g. https://fermata-cdn.<username>.workers.dev)
CDN_URL=https://fermata-cdn.your-username.workers.dev
```

---

## 2. Cloudflare Worker Setup (One-Time)

Run these commands in an empty directory on your machine to deploy the proxy:

### 1. Initialize Project
```bash
# Install Wrangler CLI globally
npm install -g wrangler

# Log in to your Cloudflare account
wrangler login

# Create a project folder and initialize
mkdir fermata-cdn-worker
cd fermata-cdn-worker
wrangler init -y
```

### 2. Add Worker Code
Replace the contents of `src/index.js` (or `src/index.ts`) with:

```javascript
export default {
  async fetch(request, env, ctx) {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    
    // Construct the direct Backblaze B2 URL
    const B2_ORIGIN = `https://f000.backblazeb2.com/file/${env.B2_BUCKET_NAME}`;
    const b2Url = new URL(url.pathname, B2_ORIGIN);
    
    // Standard Cloudflare cache key (ignores query parameters like ?t=...)
    const cacheKey = new Request(b2Url.toString(), request);
    const cache = caches.default;
    
    // Try serving from Cloudflare edge cache first
    let response = await cache.match(cacheKey);
    
    if (!response) {
      // Cache miss: Fetch from Backblaze B2 (zero egress fees apply)
      response = await fetch(b2Url.toString());
      
      // Cache successful requests only
      if (response.status === 200 && request.method === "GET") {
        const headers = new Headers(response.headers);
        headers.set("Cache-Control", "public, max-age=31536000"); // Cache for 1 year
        headers.set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*");         // Enable CORS for players
        
        response = new Response(response.body, {
          status: response.status,
          headers
        });
        
        // Put in cache in the background
        ctx.waitUntil(cache.put(cacheKey, response.clone()));
      }
    }
    
    return response;
  }
};
```

### 3. Add Wrangler Configuration
Edit your `wrangler.toml` file to include your B2 Bucket Name variable:
```toml
name = "fermata-cdn"
main = "src/index.js"
compatibility_date = "2024-01-01"

[vars]
B2_BUCKET_NAME = "fermata-music-app"
```

### 4. Deploy
```bash
wrangler deploy
```
*Note down the generated Worker URL (e.g. `https://fermata-cdn.<your-username>.workers.dev`).*

---

## 3. Configure Cache Rules (Highly Recommended)
By default, Cloudflare will run your Worker code for every incoming request. To protect your **100,000 free requests per day** limit:
1. Go to your **Cloudflare Dashboard**.
2. Navigate to your **Workers** application dashboard.
3. Add a **Cache Rule** (under Rules > Cache Rules) matching your Worker domain path:
   * **Matching Criteria**: `Host` equals `fermata-cdn.<username>.workers.dev`
   * **Action**: **Cache Everything** (or set Edge Cache TTL to 1 month / 1 year).
4. Cloudflare's CDN will now intercept matching hits and serve files directly, bypassing Worker execution counts entirely.
